session_result: SL hit intrabar, price recovered after
macro_context: Weekend thin liquidity; BTC/broader crypto risk-on bounce continuing (weak US jobs data -> dovish Fed read), no SOL-specific catalyst; DXY/VIX stale at Friday close
execution_notes: SL 80.30 breached intrabar on the M15 09:15-09:30 UTC candles (low 79.68), well after the trw2-crypto-sun-am 02:30 UK check showed price only ~0.47-0.48 above SL. Price recovered to ~80.91 by the trw2-crypto-sun-mid 14:00 UK check but the stop was already triggered intrabar - counted as closed at SL regardless of the later recovery.
lesson_learned: A deteriorating setup sitting <1% above SL between routine checks can still get swept intrabar well before the next scheduled check (~12h gap between 02:30 and 14:00 here) - the deterioration was correctly flagged last run but the check cadence was not tight enough to catch the actual stop-out in real time.
setup_quality_after_close: HTF direction call was reasonable (D1 still net bullish, +9.56%/5d) but the LTF entry OB (80.87-81.38) failed to hold and price swept fully through SL before recovering - entry timing/structure read was the weak point, not the HTF thesis.
improvement_note: Consider an interim/tighter check cadence (or an automated alert) for any setup flagged as sitting within ~1% of its SL, rather than waiting a full ~12h for the next scheduled routine.

session_result: Entry filled and SL hit within the same M15 bar (14:30-14:45 UTC / 15:30-15:45 UK), price recovered afterward but stop was triggered first
macro_context: No high-impact USD/GBP/EUR news at time of move; DXY 100.936 flat, VIX 15.51 risk-on cooling - no macro conflict, this was a pure structural failure of the OB/OTE zone under a sharp downside wick
execution_notes: Bar-walk M15 from last_checked_at 2026-07-10T14:15:00Z found bar 1783693800 (14:30-14:45 UTC): open 4105.46, high 4107.895, low 4072.8, close 4099.235, volume 25977 (3-4x avg vs surrounding 5-14K bars). Bar range (4072.8-4107.895) spans both the entry zone (4090.00-4093.50) and SL (4073). OHLC sequence is inferable and not truly ambiguous: open 4105.46 sits above the entry zone, so price fell from the open through 4090-4093.50 (filling the LONG) before continuing down to the 4072.8 low (breaching SL 4073), then reversed to close 4099.235. No TP1 (4133) or TP2 (4148) touch at any point before or after - max high in the following bars only reached 4116.465. Entry_mid 4091.75, SL 4073 (risk 18.75pts). Consistent with the sig-084 precedent: an intrabar SL sweep followed by a price recovery still counts as SL hit, not a miss.
lesson_learned: Third consecutive loss for a technically well-reasoned Setup A sweep-reversal entry (confidence 86, tight OB/OTE/liquidity confluence) - the entry zone sat directly in the path of a single violent 25pt wick that swept clean through both the fill and the stop in one 15m candle. The structural read (sweep+CHoCH+bounce) was correct in direction but the SL (beyond the OB/FVG low) wasn't wide enough to survive the immediate continuation of the same volatility spike that created the entry signal.
setup_quality_after_close: Thesis direction ultimately proved right (price held above 4085 lows after this bar and continued higher in subsequent bars) but the execution window was too tight - entering right at the edge of an active high-volatility sweep left no room for the SL before the same volatility continued past it.
improvement_note: Consider requiring at least one full bar of post-sweep consolidation/cooldown before publishing a sweep-reversal entry when the triggering move itself is still high-volume/high-volatility (25977 vol vs ~7-14K baseline) - entering immediately into the tail of an active volatility spike compresses SL survivability.

session_result: Entry filled and SL hit within the same M15 bar (17:30-17:45 UTC / 18:30-18:45 UK, 2026-07-15), price swept up through the 4047-4054 entry zone and continued to a high of 4066.295 before reversing, closing the bar at 4063.045
macro_context: Discovered during an unusually long-running trw2-london session that spanned real wall-clock time from 2026-07-15 08:30 UK through 2026-07-16 ~08:45 UK; the scheduled trw2-newyork/trw2-ny2/trw2-daily-close routines for 2026-07-15 never fired against this repo in the interim (confirmed via untouched journal/model_portfolio file mtimes), so this resolution was caught late by this session's own freshness re-check rather than by the normal next-routine bar-walk
execution_notes: Bar-walk M15 100+ bars from published_at 2026-07-15T07:42:00Z through 2026-07-16T07:45:00Z. Bar 1784118600 (open 4039.6, high 4066.295, low 4039.425, close 4063.045, volume 21767 - 3x avg) is the first bar to touch either level: price rose from open 4039.6 (below the entry zone) through 4047-4054 (filling the SHORT) and continued to the bar high 4066.295, breaching SL 4065, before falling back to close 4063.045. Unambiguous ordering (price path from below can only reach SL by first passing through the lower entry zone). No TP1/TP2 touch at any point before this bar. Entry_mid 4050.5, SL 4065 (risk 14.5pts).
lesson_learned: Second consecutive Setup B continuation SHORT loss on XAU following a similar pattern to sig-085 (entry fill + SL hit within the same volatile M15 bar) - the pending/future entry-box pattern (publish before the pullback happens) makes the entry vulnerable to a single high-volume impulse candle sweeping straight through both entry and SL zones without any intervening structure to slow it down. Worth considering a minimum-bar-count confirmation (e.g. require at least one M15 close back below the entry zone after the retracement, before validating the short) for future Setup B continuation entries where the entry and SL are close together (here only 11pts / 0.27% apart).
setup_quality_after_close: HTF read (D1/H4 background bearish, H1 BOS chain) was directionally reasonable and the confluence zone (FVG+BOS+fib 0.618) was genuine, but the entry-to-SL distance (11pts) was too tight relative to the volatility that materialized during the NY session - a single 3x-avg-volume impulse candle erased the setup before any LTF confirmation of rejection could develop.
improvement_note: For Setup B continuation entries with entry-to-SL distance under ~15pts on XAU, consider requiring at least one M15 candle to close back below the entry zone (confirming rejection) before the setup is treated as filled/valid, rather than accepting the first touch of the zone as an automatic fill.
